weather.summary: Saturday: 10°C, overcast clouds. Sunday: 15°C, broken clouds

metadata.greeting: This weekend is packed with exciting opportunities for your little one! From free museum adventures to a puppet show and creative activities at the Southbank Centre, there's something for every interest and energy level. With great weather on Sunday, be sure to take advantage of the many parks and outdoor activities available in Hackney and beyond!

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service.
activities.whyItWorks: Free, drop-in, and suitable for a wide age range, allowing siblings to enjoy together. Books stimulate imagination.

transport.fromHackney: Various buses to Hackney Central, then a short walk
transport.tip: Check Hackney Showroom website for term dates.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: A museum designed specifically for children, with hands-on activities, workshops, and exhibitions.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers sensory play, unlimited visits with a ticket, and encourages imagination and design.

transport.fromHackney: Central line to Bethnal Green
transport.tip: Check for special exhibitions and workshops online.

activities.title: Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park
activities.description: Splash about in the fountains and playgrounds.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers space to move, outdoor play, and is suitable for all ages.

transport.fromHackney: Various buses to Stratford or Overground to Hackney Wick then walk.
transport.tip: Pack a change of clothes for fountain play!

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: Small and beautifully designed wooden playground with a sweet pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: Specifically designed for toddlers, safe and engaging.

transport.fromHackney: Various buses to Stratford or Overground to Hackney Wick then walk.
transport.tip: Located within the larger Olympic Park, so plenty of space to explore.

activities.title: Victoria Park
activities.description: Ponds and children's play areas with giant slides and a paddling pool.
activities.whyItWorks: Local, free, and offers a variety of activities including playgrounds and space to run.

transport.fromHackney: Walk, bus, or cycle
transport.tip: Check for events happening in the park.

activities.title: Museum of the Home
activities.description: Explore the history of homes and domestic life.
activities.whyItWorks: Engaging exhibits for all ages, sparking curiosity about the past.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Hoxton
transport.tip: Check for family activities and workshops.

activities.title: Gods Own Junkyard
activities.description: Neon signs and more in a gallery, alongside a cute cafe.
activities.whyItWorks: Visually stimulating and colourful, with a relaxed cafe for breaks.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Walthamstow Central
transport.tip: Check opening times before visiting.

activities.title: Big Penny Social Easter Family Disco
activities.description: Dance to family-friendly tunes, enjoy games and singalongs, and then take part in the egg hunt.
activities.whyItWorks: Great way for kids to burn energy dancing and having fun. Egg hunt is an added bonus.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Walthamstow Central
transport.tip: Check website for ticket prices and times.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Explore iconic objects and hands-on exhibits, including the Apollo 10 and the first Apple computer.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive exhibits, space for kids to explore, and caters to different interests.

transport.fromHackney: Central line to Notting Hill Gate, then Circle/District line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Book tickets for Wonderlab in advance to avoid disappointment.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore the history of planet Earth, including dinosaurs and interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive exhibits, visually stimulating, and perfect for curious minds.

transport.fromHackney: Central line to Notting Hill Gate, then Circle/District line to South Kensington
transport.tip: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.price: Free (Aquarium and Butterfly House have fees)
activities.description: Wide range of exhibits, gardens, animal walk, and aquarium.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a mix of indoor and outdoor activities, animals, and hands-on experiences.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Forest Hill
transport.tip: Check website for family events and workshops.

activities.title: The Very Hungry Caterpillar Show
activities.description: Adaptation of Eric Carle's books with over 75 puppets.
activities.whyItWorks: Colorful puppets and familiar stories will capture the attention of young children.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Blackheath
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: British Museum
activities.description: Explore two million years of human history and culture.
activities.whyItWorks: Vast collection, activity backpacks and explorer trails available for kids.

transport.fromHackney: Central line to Holborn or Tottenham Court Road
transport.tip: Pick up an activity backpack from the Family Desk.

activities.title: Southbank Centre
activities.description: Riverside hub of arts, fun, and food with concerts, workshops, and family activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Welcoming, accessible, and packed with energy, with many free activities.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Liverpool Street, then Central line to Waterloo
transport.tip: Check the events calendar before heading out.

activities.title: Tate Modern
activities.description: Diverse collections of modern and contemporary art with a kids' room with interactive screens.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive exhibits, free, and visually stimulating.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then bus to Southwark Street
transport.tip: Check for family workshops and activities.

activities.title: Sky Garden
activities.description: Stunning indoor garden with panoramic views of the city.
activities.whyItWorks: Unique experience, free, and offers great views.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Liverpool Street, then a short walk
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Roam around the gardens and find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues to win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor activity, chocolate reward, and fun for all ages.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Waterloo, then train to Hampton Court
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, especially for Easter weekend.

activities.title: Octonauts Above & Beyond at Kew Gardens
activities.description: An Octonauts-themed trail through Kew Gardens with simple missions.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines outdoor exploration with a popular children's series.

transport.fromHackney: District line to Kew Gardens
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Wander past giraffes, lions, monkeys and more incredible wildlife as you search for hidden eggs.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines animals with a fun Easter egg hunt.

transport.fromHackney: Bus to Regent's Park
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Discovery Days: Spring Surprises
activities.description: Free sessions celebrating the start of spring with simple, nature-based activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Free, nature-based, and hands-on.

transport.fromHackney: Central line to Lancaster Gate or Marble Arch
transport.tip: Check website for specific activity times.

activities.title: Peppa Pig's egg-stra special Easter Afternoon Tea Bus Tour
activities.description: Peppa Pig themed afternoon tea on a London bus with Easter travel cup and Easter Egg hunt activity.
activities.whyItWorks: Fun theme, combines sightseeing with afternoon tea.

transport.fromHackney: Various pick-up points, check website.
transport.tip: Book in advance.

activities.title: Easter Egg Trail at Cutty Sark
activities.price: Free with entry to the ship
activities.description: Discover facts and find hidden eggs, unscramble a secret nautical code and claim a chocolatey treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines history with an Easter egg hunt.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Cutty Sark
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: REPLAY: A Limitless Recycled Playground
activities.description: A play space made out of repurposed materials.
activities.whyItWorks: Creative and engaging play space made of recycled materials is both educational and fun.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Liverpool Street, then Central line to Waterloo
transport.tip: Check website for time slots and book in advance.

activities.title: Sneaky Squirrel's Chocolate Chase
activities.description: Immersive adventure for Easter.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive story, tailored for young children, involves books and Easter theme.

transport.fromHackney: Overground to Finsbury Park, then bus to Crouch End
transport.tip: Check website for specific dates and times.
